notes: Three back-to-back no-op turns ('Reply with exactly the word: ok') in ONE live vibe REPL session, locking down the multi-turn TOKEN ACCUMULATOR. RE-ASSESSED after c37c6830: the vibe parser.go emitContribution now emits, on every turn_done, a PerTurnContribution carrying the DELTA of the session-cumulative prompt/completion token counts (sidecar.go now reads stats.session_prompt_tokens / stats.session_completion_tokens), so the monotonic SessionMetrics.TotalTokens accumulation the scenario's acceptance requires now reaches events.jsonl (verified end-to-end through the production tailer in metrics_test.go: CumInputTokens/CumOutputTokens accumulate). daemon=full, NOT known_failing. tokens_nonzero=true. COST CAVEAT: cost is still not emitted (cost_nonzero=false) — ProviderCostUSD is deliberately unset and mistral-medium-3.5 has no USD-per-token price in core/pkg/capacity (only a CO2 factor); cost computes once mistral pricing lands (follow-up unlock 1.8). Cost is not in the scenario's acceptance_criteria, so it is a caveat, not a bug. CACHE CAVEAT: the cache-bucket sub-assertion (CacheReadTokens>0, fresh input=0) is agent-side unavailable — Vibe's stats expose no cache_read field, so it is unobservable regardless of a parser fix. The phase DSL asserts only the three ready->working->ready cycles on ONE session that the accounting rides on (the accumulation claim has no metric form in the phase DSL). Single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<...> transcript directory, sessionIDFromPath), modeled like claudecode/basic-turn: birth anchored to start unpinned; every turn phase pins to the birth session via same_session_as so a co-occurring proc-<PID> presession can never satisfy them. Recipe needs wait_turn, still stubbed by the scaffolded driver (driver=gap:wait_turn); record ports it before driving. Model mistral-medium-3.5 confirmed from live config.active_model. BACKFLOW (re-spec): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter — birth re-templated as presession_birth->session_birth(new_session) from the codex sibling (a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready before the real session_<id> transcript dir appears, then reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id); every post-birth phase pins same_session_as session_birth. OBSERVATIONS: the offline replay golden cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so model and tokens are UNVERIFIABLE offline — like kiro-cli the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false).

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
